title: arabis holboellii var. collinsii
intro: Arabis holboellii var. collinsii, also known as Collins' rockcress, is a variety of flowering plant in the mustard family, characterized by its white to pale purple flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Collins' rockcress are attributed to its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Arabis holboellii var. collinsii can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
